description: Incoming viscosity check at Ford Dearborn showed kinematic viscosity at 100C reading 8.0 cSt on two of five drums sampled, below the 8.2 cSt minimum required by WSS-M2C961-A1. Remaining three drums tested at 8.3-8.4 cSt within specification.
rootCause: Additive injection pump calibration drift on Blending Line A caused under-treat of viscosity index improver in first portion of batch. Pump recalibrated and in-process sampling frequency increased from every 2000 gallons to every 500 gallons.
resolution: Two non-conforming drums replaced with conforming product from batch BN-2026-0235 at no charge. Credit of $1,820 issued for production line downtime. Corrective action report filed.

description: Only 1,500 of 2,000 gallons ordered were delivered. Partial shipment received with no advance notification of short ship. Production line scheduling was disrupted requiring emergency procurement from alternate supplier.
rootCause: Inventory management system failed to flag low stock condition on ATK-MWF-220 at Golden Valley. Sales accepted order against insufficient inventory without checking real-time levels.
resolution: Remaining 500 gallons shipped expedited at Ascentek cost. Inventory alert threshold raised from 1,500 to 2,000 gallons. Real-time inventory check added to order acceptance workflow.

description: Ford purchasing flagged that Certificate of Analysis for in-progress batch BN-2026-0293 has not been received per contractual requirement of 48 hours prior to shipment. Delivery date of 2026-03-21 is at risk if CoA is not provided by 2026-03-19.
rootCause: Batch still in production (status: in-progress). CoA cannot be generated until final QC testing is complete. Communication gap between production scheduling and customer service on expected completion date.

description: Slight haze observed in three totes upon receipt at Ford Louisville Assembly. Product met all chemical and physical specifications on re-test but visual appearance did not meet Ford incoming inspection standards for clarity.
rootCause: Product was stored in outdoor staging area at Golden Valley for 36 hours during temperature swing from 45F to 18F, causing temporary wax crystal formation in semi-synthetic base stock. Product cleared upon warming to 70F.
resolution: Totes were held at 70F ambient for 24 hours and re-inspected as clear. Written procedure updated to prohibit outdoor staging of 0W-20 products below 35F. No product replacement required.
